biases.name: Confirmation Bias
biases.definition: Seeking or interpreting information that confirms existing beliefs while discounting contradictory evidence.

biases.name: Anchoring Bias
biases.definition: Over-relying on the first piece of information encountered when making judgments.
detectionSignals: first number or impression drove the decision

biases.name: Fundamental Attribution Error
biases.definition: Overemphasizing personality-based explanations for others' behavior while underemphasizing situational factors.

biases.name: Hindsight Bias
biases.definition: Believing past events were more predictable after they have already occurred.

biases.name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
biases.definition: Continuing a course of action because of previously invested resources rather than future value.
detectionSignals: already invested too much to stop
